Tests the length property of the arguments object in a JavaScript function.

# Purpose
This code is a simple script that demonstrates the use of the `arguments` object in JavaScript. The function [`f_arg`](<#04-003f_arg>) takes two parameters, `x` and `y`, but it does not use them directly. Instead, it returns the `arguments` object, which contains all the arguments passed to the function. The script includes an assertion that checks if the length of the `arguments` object is zero when [`f_arg`](<#04-003f_arg>) is called without any arguments. This illustrates how the `arguments` object behaves when no parameters are provided to the function.

### f\_arg<!-- {{#callable:pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.04/11.02.04-003.f_arg}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../../../../../../src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/11/11.02/11.02.04/11.02.04-003.js#L17>)

Returns the `arguments` object containing all arguments passed to the function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `x`: The first argument passed to the function.
    - `y`: The second argument passed to the function.
- **Control Flow**:
    - The function receives two parameters, `x` and `y`.
    - The function returns the `arguments` object, which is an array-like object containing all arguments passed to the function.
- **Output**: The `arguments` object, which includes all arguments passed to the function.
